description: Evaluation taxonomy for cyber threat intelligence (CTI) quality and conversion quality in workflows such as MISP/STIX exchange and CTI Transmute, covering relevance, accuracy, timeliness, clarity, specificity, format validity, conversion fidelity, and usefulness.

predicates.description: Overall weighted assessment of the CTI artifact quality.
Whether the CTI pertains directly to user mission and decision-making needs.
Whether assertions are based on reliable, verified, and corroborated data.
Whether CTI is delivered with enough lead time for effective action.
Whether CTI is understandable, unambiguous, and actionable for intended audiences.
Whether CTI contains concrete details (what, where, when, who, how) needed to act.
Practical utility of CTI for operations, detection, response, or strategic decisions.
Conformance of CTI artifacts to expected schema/syntax (e.g., STIX/MISP structures).
How faithfully intelligence survives format conversion (e.g., MISP to STIX and back).
Reliability of primary and secondary sources underpinning the CTI.
Strength and sufficiency of supporting evidence for the claims.
Analyst or reviewer confidence in the CTI judgments.

Strong quality for this dimension; minor limitations may remain. Overall weighted assessment of the CTI artifact quality.
Excellent quality for this dimension; fully or near-fully meets expectations. Overall weighted assessment of the CTI artifact quality.
